name: Aerospec
description: A mesh sensor network capable of simultaneously monitoring air quality across multiple locations, offering a solution to air quality control in hospitals, production sites, or fire-affected areas.

name: Ionosphere Detection
description: The Sphere Very-Low-Frequency (VLF) Antenna System project develops a globally deployable, high-performance VLF antenna system to monitor and analyze signals from meteors, rocket launches, lightning, and weather-related events.

name: Industrial Training and Assessment Center
description: The ITAC sub-team generates findings to improve facilities' energy consumption and productivity through hands-on industrial energy assessments.

name: Pacific Northwest Building Training and Assessment Center
description: PNW BTAC trains students in best practices for building performance and energy efficiency in commercial and institutional buildings, particularly in traditionally underserved communities.

name: SINEW Mask
description: The Surface Ionization Non-contact Extended Wear (SINEW) Mask offers a dynamic solution with its ergonomic design and use of efficient electrohydrodynamic (EHD)-based filtration.

name: Air Leaks
description: Automates the detection of leaks in pressurized air and gas systems using ultrasonic vibration sensors, reducing energy waste and maintenance costs for industrial facilities.

name: WEGO
description: A Microsoft Office Word ribbon tool for technical writing that enables writers to easily insert figures and equations into their Word documents with precision and speed.

name: ECoS
description: A technical writing tool that helps writers with document collaboration and learning the complex expectations of professional and academic writing.

name: Fruit Ripeness
description: A sensor that measures fruit skin impedance to determine ripeness, providing farmers and distributors with actionable data to optimize food handling, reduce waste, and lower operating costs.

name: Ammonia
description: Focuses on ammonia concentration monitoring by developing single-walled carbon nanotube (SWCNT) sensors — a low-cost alternative offering approximately ten times the sensitivity and accuracy of commercial products.
